text.title: Agent GA-Readiness Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-ga-readiness-checklist enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Checklist has ≥12 items grouped: auth, rate-limits, billing, eval, runbook, rollback, kill switch, cost ceiling, on-call rota, incident channel, data retention, ToS+privacy.
rule.rationale: Fewer items leave a gap; more items dilute and get skipped. 12 covers the GA-failure modes seen in 2026.

rule.statement: Every item is either checked DONE with evidence OR explicitly SKIPPED with a written one-line reason.
rule.rationale: Blank items = silent skips; auditable skips force the team to acknowledge the gap.

rule.statement: Eval bar item requires last 5 nightly runs all green and pass-rate ≥85% (or team-defined threshold).
rule.rationale: Single green run is luck; 5 in a row signals stability.

rule.statement: Rollback path is tested end-to-end before GA flip; not as a week-1 deliverable.
rule.rationale: Untested rollback fails when it's most needed; pre-flip test catches wiring bugs.

rule.statement: Checklist is dated + signed by owner; the artefact lives at `releases/<date>-ga-readiness.md`.
rule.rationale: Versioned, signed artefacts make incident post-analysis tractable.
